g.text: Claude Code drives heso via the existing skill. No new agent loop.
: use heso to find three rust web scraping libraries and summarize them
Calling Claude Code (claude -p) from the repo root.
It picks up skills/heso/SKILL.md and uses heso verbs as its tools.
command="heso search \"rust web scraping librar... description="Search via heso CLI"
command="cargo run -q --bin heso -- search \"ru... description="Run heso search via cargo...
command="cargo run -q --bin heso -- search \"ru... description="Run heso search via cargo...
command="ls target/debug/heso* target/release/h... description="Check for built heso bina...
command="./target/release/heso.exe search \"rus... description="Run heso search" timeout=...
Used `heso search` to query DuckDuckGo. Top three Rust web scraping libraries from the results:
1. **reqwest** — async HTTP client. The fetch half of most Rust scrapers; pairs with tokio for
concurrency. Handles TLS, redirects, cookies, gzip out of the box. Doesn't parse HTML — you bring your own
2. **scraper** — HTML parser built on `html5ever` with CSS-selector queries (jQuery-style
`select(&selector)`). The canonical pick for extracting data from a fetched page. Synchronous, ergonomic,
3. **spider** (`spider-rs/spider`) — full crawler framework, not just a fetcher/parser. Low-latency
concurrent crawling, optional headless Chrome for JS-rendered pages, AI-agent oriented. Use when you need
to traverse a site, not just hit one URL.
Typical stack: `reqwest + scraper + tokio` for static pages; `spider` when you need crawling or JS
